title: erythrophleum teysmannii
intro: Erythrophleum teysmannii, also known as the "Tawari tree," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines. The medicinal properties of Erythrophleum teysmannii are attributed to its roots, which contain a compound called erythrophleine, a toxic alkaloid with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections. Erythrophleum teysmannii is traditionally prepared as a herbal decoction or infusion, and its roots can also be processed into a tincture or extract for medicinal purposes.
